calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-08-2025 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2025 Set For Trial On Sep-08-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment And For Sanctions Pursuant To Crc 2.30; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Samuel Ray
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Robert Kennard, Esq. 916-920-2295 Nelson & Kennard for Plaintiff.
Samuel Ray, Esq. 415-579-1413 Colla & Ray LLP for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court admonishes parties. Parties did not inform the Court they had a settlement.
Counsel for Plaintiff Ms. Kennard argues. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruing as follows:

DEFENDANT NASIR PATEL's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment And For Sanctions Pursuant To Crc 2.30; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Samuel Ray.

Defendant Nasir Patel's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment And For Sanctions Pursuant To CRC 2.30 to which no opposition has been filed is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Defendant Nasir Patel has shown default and default judgment were entered due to mistake, surprise or excusable neglect. The motion itself states it is made by "Defendant Highfield Hospitality, Inc." (Mot.at p. 2.) But the caption, the footer and the text of the MPA make clear it is made by Defendant Naris Patel. All communications from counsel to Plaintiff's were on behalf of Nasir Patel. (See Ray Decl., passim.) Notably, Nasir Patel is the only defendant currently in the action and there is not and never has been a defendant in this action by the name Highfield Hospitality, Inc. Further, Plaintiff forfeited any objection on these grounds when it failed to file an opposition to the motion.

The request for sanctions is denied because it is derivative of the set-aside motion, which fails. Additionally, the moving party failed to comply with the requirements of Rule 2.30 when it failed in the "motion" to state " the applicable rule that has been violated," describe "the specific conduct that is alleged to have violated the rule" or "identify the attorney [or] law firm - against whom sanctions are sought." It is not sufficient to state that counsel (generally) failed to comply with "applicable rules." (See Mot. at p. 2.) The court cannot and is unwilling to overlook these infirmities.

Mr. Ray to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's ruling to the contestdept302tr@sftc.org ASAP.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
